title: Scam
text:
Revolut upgraded me to a Platinum/Gold plan and clearly offered two months of Premium completely free, with no charges mentioned. When I pressed “order card,” there was absolutely NO information shown about express delivery or any £17.99 fee.

After the free period ended, Revolut suddenly charged me £17.99. When I challenged this, they claimed it was for express delivery. This is outrageous and unacceptable, because at no point during the order process was I informed that I was choosing express delivery or that I would be charged £17.99 for it.

This is a hidden charge, and it feels like a deliberate scam. Taking money without clearly disclosing the cost upfront is misleading and dishonest. Customers should not be charged for something they never knowingly agreed to.

title: i got scam
text:
I had a very disappointing experience with the Revolut app and their customer support.

I was scammed and immediately contacted their support team, providing clear evidence and detailed information about the fraud and the scammer. I expected at least some level of investigation or protection as a customer, but I felt completely ignored. No real action was taken regarding my case.

Instead of helping me recover or block the fraudulent activity, I was left with no resolution. Even worse, I was then contacted about charges related to the money I had been scammed out of, which made the situation feel even more unfair and stressful.

As a customer, I trusted Revolut to provide support in situations like this, especially when fraud is involved. Unfortunately, my experience has made me lose confidence in their customer protection process.

I hope they improve how they handle scam reports and take stronger action to protect users in the future.

title: Hidden Fees and Unresolved Support Issues
text:
I’ve been a Revolut customer for over 5 years, but this is the first time I’ve felt so disappointed. I was charged unexpected fees after a single €100 withdrawal in January. I understood there might be a fee for withdrawing cash on credit, but nowhere did Revolut show or explain how much interest would apply. Then came random charges of €6.80 in February and €9.24 in March with no clear reason.
When I contacted support, I got conflicting responses, one said I set up an instalment plan (I didn’t), another said I didn’t pay my statement (untrue, I have automatic payments). After several chats, no one could clearly explain the charges.
Revolut needs more transparency and reliable customer service. Taking money from long-time customers without clear justification is unacceptable.

title: Poor Customer Service and Fee Farming
text: It took almost 5 business days for a simple bank transfer to deposit in the account and to get it “instantly” you are paying a ludicrous fee. Additionally, good luck if you ever have a dispute. Almost entirely a chatbot and all it does is point you to file a police report no matter how small the transaction is. There are far better options out there and I highly suggest an alternative.

title: Waste of time / dysfunctional app
text:
Knowing this company is crypto friendly, I figured I would give them a shot. I didn't realize how low quality the company is. Their app is complete trash. Why they force you to go through that to initially start your account, instead of allowing you to go through the website, is beyond me

He can only verify certain types of IDs. For anyone else that lives in Florida, don't waste your time. It can't verify a Florida ID. I wasted my time trying to submit mine and eight different freaking times. Every single time, it rejected it.

If they are going to require an ID like that, they should have all ideas from all states in their system!!! for them to not do so is not only extremely irresponsible, but also a real easy way to take off a potential customer and get a negative review like this. And I have seen multiple other people complain about it not being able to read their IDs, so I am sure it is not only Florida drivers licenses that they have issues with

In case that wasn't bad enough, I couldn't even log into my account normally, because despite the fact that I know very well exactly what password I put into their system, their system somehow took in the wrong password!!! I knew exactly what password I put in, but yet it would repeatedly tell me that it was the wrong password!!!  and their "password Recovery" is completely worthless as well

Their app is trash, it's sloppy, it's incomplete, and with all of that said, I would not trust this company at all whatsoever. They are clearly slopped together. Then to make it worse, their customer service is pretty much nonexistent, no fraud department, you are pretty much just left to fend for yourself when it comes to this account.

Find an alternative. This one isn't worth the hassle.

title: Excellent Banking Experience!

I’ve been using revolut for over a year so far, The app is incredibly user friendly packed with useful features and makes banking seamless.
-Sending and receive money is very quick and easy

-the disposable cards help me avoid giving unnecessary information to sketchy sites.
-I haven’t gone overseas yet but you can quickly, and easily exchange currency with great exchange rates through the app.
-getting instant transaction notification gives me a peace of mind.

Revolut is miles ahead of traditional brick and mortar banks when it comes to their banking app.

Avoid if you value financial security.

title: My experience with Revolut support:
text:
My experience with Revolut support:
My account was closed previously due to opening multiple accounts in the past, and I tried to contact support to explain my situation and request a chance to have one single personal account.
The first support agent I spoke with was Sahibzada, who promised to get back to me within 10–15 minutes, but I waited about 30 minutes and received no response.
Then another agent, Kevin, contacted me. I sent him a brief summary of my issue, but he also did not respond after several minutes.

Result: I did not receive any solution to my problem, and my time was wasted.
Unfortunately, I felt that the service was poor and ineffective, and my request was not handled professionally.

title: Größter Müll ever !!!
text:
Ich habe mir die App heruntergeladen, weil ich dachte, dass sie sehr vertrauenswürdig ist. Ich habe zwei Konzertkarten gekauft, und die Verkäuferin wollte, dass ich über Revolut bezahle. Das habe ich dann auch gemacht – leider war es ein Scam.
Daraufhin habe ich sofort Revolut kontaktiert, einen Fall eröffnet und nachgefragt, ob man die Zahlung nachverfolgen und mir das Geld zurückerstatten kann. title: Tolle Plattform - für größere Beträge nur erschwert nutzbar
text:
Für kleine Beträge (bis ca 20t€) sehr nett. 
Wenn man größere Beträge bewegt, kommt als erstes die Compliance Nachfrage nach der Herkunft der Gelder - gut, habe ich Verständnis für. Wenn ich dann z.B. 100t€ auf mein Brokerdepot nach Singapur überweise kommt eine automatisierte Betrugswarnung und ich muss ca. 20x versichern, dass ich "weiß, was ich tue". Zeitaufwand: 10 min. Ok. Dann wurde ich vom Service kontakiert und musste nochmals gefühlt die gleichen Fragen beantworten, screenshots meines Depot hochladen, Versicherungen über meine Erfahrungen im Investmentbereich abgeben (Zeitaufwand: eine Sunde +). Am störensten habe ich empfunden, dass ich immer wieder das Gleiche gefragt wurde - wie in einem schlechten Krimi. Erst meine Drohung, die Geschäftsverbindung zu beenden führte schließlich zur Ausführung der Überweisung (die zugegebenermaßen dann enorm schnell und sehr günstig war). 
Eine neue Überweisung an den gleichen Zahlungsempfänger führte wieder zu der automatisierten Betrugswarnung mit den 20 Antworten. Allerdings hatte der Service dazugelehrt - nach einer einzigen Versicherung, dass die Überweisung mein freier Wille ist und einer Unterschrift wurde mit der Ausführung begonnen (wieder enorm schnell & günstig). 
Es ergibt sich also in meinem usecase ein gemischtes Bild: Insgesamt finde ich Revolut sehr gut & innovativ und kann es sehr empfehlen - mit der Einschränkung, dass das Handling von größeren Summen eine wahre Geduldsprobe darstellt. Das klappt mit Wise wesentlich besser.

title: The bank can not resolve the technical problem for more than 1 month
text:
In beginning of March, I lost access to using cryptocurrency because my information was under the check. Apparently, nothing was wrong with it — some technical problem appeared. After one month, nothing was solved — supports just repeated words „soon“, „priority“ and „I am sorry“. In the end, apparently, the technical team „solved“ the bug but some clients (like unlucky me) still faced the same problem. No one reached me one and no one checked if my account is not affected till I wrote to the support one more time. I can not even transfer my funds back — fully locked. 

I am a political refugee and work for the state — but maybe bank think that I am a danger for them or not important.

text: Da ich zu meinem Sparkassen Konto nur eine Girocard habe, die in physischen Läden zwar sehr gut funktioniert aber beim Onlineshopping per Apple Pay manchmal Probleme bereitet hat, war ich auf der Suche nach einer Lösung und bin auf Revolut gestoßen. Zusätzlich gibt es die Möglichkeit mit einer Einmalkarte zu bezahlen, wenn man einem Händler nicht traut oder man sonstige Bedenken hat. Da ich dieses Konto immer per Sofortüberweisung mit dem benötigten Betrag auflade (Dauer ca. 30 Sekunden) und die blaue Karte ansonsten eingefroren bei 0,00 € habe ist das die perfekte Lösung für mich.

title: Excellent, with room to improve
text:
I have used a Revolut for about a year now, and it is by far the easiest, most robust and pleasurable e-banking app to use out of Lydia, Bunq, SG, Wise and Boursorama. The interface is clear, and the app is responsive. However, there are a few points that should be improved:
- Adding money to Vaults is not counted as an actual transaction, and so are not currently counted in the budget despite being labelled as such. This seems like an easy fix. 
- It is not possible to categorise transaction granularly. Example: a payment at a store where some of the items would be in one category and others would be in another can not be split as such. Only one category is possible, it would be great to be able to assign amounts to within a single transaction. 
- Related in this is the ability to assign a certain portion of a transaction to a category and exclude the rest. 
- References are not including when exporting statements. 

Cheers for the great service! Long live Revolut 🤗
